problem: Can superconductivity be achieved at room temperature — what material structure?
mechanism: Optical rectenna for direct photon-to-electron conversion
hypothesis: Can superconductivity be achieved at room temperature — what material structure? remains unsolved because previous research missed the role of Optical. Optical rectenna for direct photon-to-electron conversion directly addresses this.

experiment.protocol: Materials characterization (XRD, SEM) with 7 month follow-up. Control group receives placebo/sham.

wisdom: In the beginner's mind there are many possibilities. In the expert's mind there are few. — Shunryu Suzuki
